description: Live Schema Parity — make the scratch-DB parity job run end-to-end. Option B (baseline/squash): adopt a schema-only snapshot of live as the forward-only replay root, archive the 122 non-replayable pre-baseline migrations, and reach a real fail-closed PASS verdict comparing the replayed baseline to live.

root_cause.ledger_not_replayable: The pre-baseline migration ledger was bidirectionally divergent from live (live had objects the repo never created; the repo had migrations live never applied; migration 202605140001_utv2_912 referenced market_universe.provider, a column that exists in neither scratch nor live). A from-scratch replay aborted partway, so parity could never reach a verdict by replaying old migrations.
root_cause.ci_connectivity: GitHub-hosted runners are IPv4-only, but Supabase direct connections (db.<ref>.supabase.co) are served over IPv6 only, so pg_dump and the parity compare's psql failed 'Network is unreachable'. Resolved by reaching live through the IPv4 session-mode Supavisor pooler via the SUPABASE_DB_POOLER_URL secret.

baseline_generation.method: pg_dump --schema-only --no-owner --no-privileges --schema=public in a postgres:17 container against the live session pooler; read-only, no data dumped, no live mutation.
baseline_generation.sanitized: Stripped pg_dump 17.x \restrict/\unrestrict psql meta-commands; made CREATE SCHEMA public idempotent. No OWNER/GRANT/ROLE/EXTENSION statements remain.
baseline_generation.partition_handling: Dynamic provider_offer_history_p<YYYYMMDD> partition children are excluded from the baseline (parent partitioned table, its management function, and provider_offer_history_compact retained).
baseline_generation.replay_validated: Installed as the sole migration and replayed from scratch via `supabase db reset` on a Supabase local stack (Postgres 17) — clean apply.

live_schema_parity.actual_label: supabase-live (project zfzdnfwdarxucxtaojxm, via IPv4 session pooler)

allowed_findings.reason: allowed_extension — enabled by default on the Supabase local stack, not on live; environment provisioning, not schema content.

allowed_extension — enabled on live for scheduled partition lifecycle, absent on the local stack (pg_cron needs shared_preload_libraries, off by default locally); environment provisioning, not schema content.

live_schema_parity.fail_closed: Drift gate is deny-by-default. Only the two named extensions are allowlisted; every relation/column/constraint/index/policy/trigger difference still FAILS the gate. A skipped/unconfigured parity run is treated as FAIL (D-CONST-4/7).

migration_reversibility: IRREVERSIBLE — the baseline is a schema snapshot, not a forward migration; its only 'down' would drop the public schema and there is no prior state to restore to (all pre-baseline migrations are archived). Recovery path is PITR. Ratified in db/migrations-rollback/irreversible-exemption-registry.json; down marker at db/migrations-rollback/00000000000000_baseline_live_schema.down.sql.

r_level_compliance.R1: pnpm test PASS — added filterSnapshot + updated parity workflow-shape test; full suite green on the branch.
r_level_compliance.R2: pnpm type-check PASS — compare-databases.ts --exclude-relation-pattern is type-safe.
r_level_compliance.R3: Live-DB runtime proof: Live Schema Parity ran end-to-end against real live Supabase (via pooler) and the Supabase local stack, drift gate verdict PASS (run 27524264024).
r_level_compliance.R4: Evidence bundle present — this file + verification.md.
r_level_compliance.R5: SHA binding set to the baseline-adoption commit 35b35b12; the only commits after it touch proof paths only.
